title: Lastrea saxicola

This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other fern species. The genus Lastrea is considered a part of the subfamily Athyrioideae, which contains several species of terrestrial and epiphytic ferns. The family Athyriaceae is often placed in the order Polypodiales, which is a large and diverse group of ferns. Within the Polypodiales order, Lastrea saxicola can be further classified based on its specific characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other species.

intro:
There is limited information on the plant Lastrea saxicola. However, I found information on Lastrea, a genus of ferns. 

Lastrea is a genus of ferns, commonly known as the shield ferns, that are native to Asia. They are known for their medicinal properties, with the most notable health benefits being the reduction of inflammation and the improvement of cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea saxicola, and other Lastrea species, include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lastrea species include teas, infusions, and dried extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, coughs, and digestive issues.
